goal: Port CK class metrics (WMC, CBO, DIT, NOC) to v2: add Language.extract_superclasses + 9 grammar overrides; add Structure.classes/methods_of/superclasses_of/weighted_methods/coupling/inheritance_depth/subclasses_of view methods; rewrite run_X_v2 for all four; cross-language parity vs legacy ck_kernel

summary.title: CK class metrics v2 port (WMC + CBO + DIT + NOC)
summary.what: Ported all four Chidamber & Kemerer 1994 metrics to v2 view-native in one intent — they share the per-corpus inheritance graph + class enumeration substrate, so building it once makes the four rules thin consumers. Added Language.extract_superclasses classmethod (per-grammar AST quirks: Python superclasses field, Java superclass+interfaces, TS class_heritage, C# base_list, C++ base_class_clause, Ruby positional <). Added ParseResult.scope_nodes (analogous to callable_nodes) and Structure._scope_node_by_key index so CBO can walk class bodies and superclasses_of can call grammar extractors. Added view methods: classes(), methods_of, superclasses_of, weighted_methods, coupling, inheritance_depth, subclasses_count. Shared helper _class_index builds parent_map / children_map / known set once per run. Parity verified against legacy ck_kernel on Python/Java/Ruby/C++ inheritance fixtures; zero mismatches across all four metrics. 475 tests pass.
summary.why: WMC/CBO/DIT/NOC are foundational structural-debt signals — high WMC = god class, high CBO = brittle coupling, deep DIT = inheritance over-extension, high NOC = abstraction over-spread. All four are structural per the slop thesis; the user confirmed 'all of these belong'. Porting them together is cheaper than four separate intents because they share the inheritance-graph substrate.
summary.user_goal: Port CK class metrics (WMC, CBO, DIT, NOC) to v2: add Language.extract_superclasses + 9 grammar overrides; add Structure.classes/methods_of/superclasses_of/weighted_methods/coupling/inheritance_depth/subclasses_of view methods; rewrite run_X_v2 for all four; cross-language parity vs legacy ck_kernel

decisions.chose: Port all four CK rules in one intent rather than splitting WMC + CBO into one intent and DIT + NOC into another.
decisions.rationale: All four consume the same _class_index (parent_map + children_map + known set). Splitting would mean building the index twice or carving artificial boundaries; bundling lets the index build once and each rule slice what it needs.

decisions.chose: Add scope_nodes to ParseResult + Structure._scope_node_by_key index.
decisions.rationale: CBO needs to walk the class body (full AST subtree of the class scope); superclasses_of needs to call extract_superclasses on the class's node. Without a scope→AST-node index, we'd have to re-walk the parse tree each lookup. Mirrors the callable_nodes pattern.

decisions.chose: Build parent_map / children_map / known set via _class_index helper, not as Structure indexes.
decisions.rationale: The inheritance graph is rule-level state, not view-level. It's needed by 3 of the 4 CK rules but not by any non-CK rule. Keeping it in class_metrics.py avoids polluting Structure with state that only CK uses.

decisions.chose: Pass parent_map / children_map / known as arguments to inheritance_depth and subclasses_count.
decisions.rationale: The view method is per-class; it shouldn't know how the corpus-wide graph was built. The rule passes the precomputed indexes in; the view does the per-class DFS / lookup against them. Keeps the view's surface narrower.

decisions.chose: Defer Go (receiver-based) and Rust (impl-based) pseudo-class handling.
decisions.rationale: Go's class story is struct + receiver-methods-from-elsewhere; Rust's is struct + impl blocks. Both need a different class-collection pattern than the body-walked grammars (legacy has separate _collect_go_classes / _collect_rust_classes). Adding that to v2 deserves its own intent; not blocking for the 7 grammars that do have body-based classes.

Legacy's _aggregate_ruby_open_classes merges Ruby class re-openings at end-of-kernel. v2 needs a similar post-pass either in the view or the rule runner. Out of scope for this initial port; documented in the commit message.

decisions.chose: Keep the cross-file simple-name conflation in NOC/DIT (children_map keyed by simple name).
decisions.rationale: Legacy does the same; the parity sweep showed matching behavior. Slop is typically run per-language so cross-language NOC inflation is rare. A future enhancement could use fully-qualified names with a known_classes restriction.

review_notes: 475 tests pass. Net -8 from removing legacy class-metrics tests + cross-language CK cases, +14 from fresh v2 tests.
Parity sweep: Python/Java/Ruby/C++ inheritance fixtures — zero mismatches on WMC, CBO, DIT, NOC vs legacy.

Port status: 11 view-native (cyclomatic, cognitive, god_module, imposters, magic_literals, volume, density, class.complexity, class.coupling, class.inheritance.depth, class.inheritance.children), 19 shim-wrapped.
Substantial Tree-internal change: ParseResult got a new field (scope_nodes), Tree._walk captures it. Backwards-compatible (default empty dict) — existing pipeline code doesn't need updates.
Known follow-ups: Go/Rust pseudo-class handling; Ruby open-class aggregation. Both flagged in commit message.

architectural_claims: Language.extract_superclasses is the canonical home for per-grammar superclass extraction. Concrete grammars override; the default is empty (no inheritance contribution).
ParseResult exposes scope_nodes mirroring callable_nodes. The view uses both via composite (path, qualname) keys.
CK metrics use a rule-level _class_index helper (parent_map + children_map + known) rather than Structure-level state. Per-rule indexes are built once per run.
behavioral_changes: All four CK class rules run view-native; identical values to legacy ck_kernel on parity-tested corpora.
Tree.scan now indexes class scope AST nodes (scope_nodes); existing consumers see the same surface plus the new index.
migration_notes: External consumers of slop.structure.rules.class_metrics: legacy run_X functions removed. The v2 entries take (Structure, RuleConfig, SlopConfig).

Go/Rust class-rule users: those grammars don't contribute to CK metrics in v2 yet — they did partially in legacy via _collect_go_classes / _collect_rust_classes. Follow-up intent will add v2 handling.
Ruby open-class users: re-opened classes are currently NOT aggregated (each declaration measured separately). Follow-up intent will add post-pass aggregation.
